sections.paragraph:
fut. יִדְאֶה apoc. יֵדֶא Psal. 18:11 , to fly; used of the rapid flight of birds of prey, Deu 28:49 Jer 48:40 49:22 of God, Psa 18:11, וַיֵדֶא עַל־כַּנְפֵי דוּחַ “and he flew upon the wings of the wind.” (For 2Ki 17:21 see נָדָא .) Hence דַּיָּה and-

דאה

f. only found in Levit. 11:14 (as in the parallel place, Deu 14:13 there is דָאָה, by mistake of transcribers); some bird of prey which flies rapidly. LXX. γύψ. Vulg. milvus. Comp. Bochart, Hieroz. t. ii. p. 191.
